comment.body: This is true, and historically, despite both formerly being communist, they were never particularly friendly. I think the west has a cloudy view and tends to think that all communist (or former) countries are allies, but that’s not the case. Just look at the conflicts between China and Vietnam. Also, look at the detente between the U.S. and China during Nixon. Russia just really wants to reassert its sphere of influence over its former states. If you understand Russians, you understand that they have no loyalty to anyone, even their own. Every person takes whatever they can.

Made 5 trades for 114k. Clean week. I think we slide down under 5500 (the VIX is about to hit spring and SPX is still distributing while top tier funds aren’t buying here) into 4/2 and then Trump most likely releases tariff bargaining announcement which gives a big relief rally into big tech ER. The dump and chop thru the summer. 
This weekend I’m studying more vol info and spending time with my boy 

Still holding 3/31 25 delta puts. Are you going to add to these Alma?

comment.body:
SPX 1hour
This just show how good Alma's mean reversion code is. 
This is the waterfall from the ATH and if you focus on the yellow mean-line one can clearly see that it's in a downword pointing slope.
But even at the green arrow where the price bounce 100 points it never turned up, but continued to point down.
But when price comes down at the blue arrow and price only moves 80 points to the upside the mean line started to point up immediately.
After that it continue it's slow upward direction.
At that time Alma said in one of her newsletter that into opex we should see oscillation and supportive flows. 
That yellow line surely show what she said - supportive

comment.body:
Well bit late to the party but here I am. Mostly I played reversion on the long side. I don't do options, only futures. And so far I'm +17% in March trading small positions. Still making too many trades but learnt to cut loses quickly and hold successful runners. 
I'm starting the day with the post, marking reversion lvls, and I'm watching both NQ (with qqq values on chart) and spx at the same time - with options flow from quantdata. And for example, AAPL had a big odte call order few minutes the whole rally began yesterday. So usually it is helpful but sometimes I find it misleading. 

If sometimes I can't figure out the bias out of the daily post, I take a break and watch, while thinking where I would take a position. And the accuracy is growing, so I feel like I'm learning in here. 😊

comment.body: I think Russians may need to get close to Yankees to get some relief, but they know deeply in their heart, US politics change along with election, which means next time or the time after time, another administration may hostile to them again. Also Europe probably won’t get along with them in any foreseeable future until they back down. Considering this, I think they may keep some distance from China to gain something from Trump, but considering future uncertainties, they won’t break up with CCP completely

comment.body:
By the way, I tell you a "secret" how can you spare hundreds if not thousands of dollars a year...

If you buy Graham - Intelligent Investor, Natenberg - Options Volatility and Pricing, Bennett - Trading Volatility and Taleb - Dynamic Hedging (very advanced), you spent 185-230 USD only once, (but note that these books are avaible online for free in PDF format)

If you are an IBKR user, real time options + futures data for equities is approximately around $600/year

Lets say you spend $97/month for SG pro, or $224/month for OD promax (including my -10% discount)

It is $3543 in a year, assuming you bought the books and didn't used the discount.
